steps.title: Spot the affirmation
steps.content: The phrase "did just that" shows that Duchamp's sculpture actually accomplished what the first sentence says he intended—confirming the intention was correct. The transition must mark this as confirmation.

steps.title: Pick the confirming word
steps.content:
The logical relationship is confirmation/emphasis — Duchamp intended to challenge what counts as art, and his sculpture did exactly that. The blank needs a transition signaling that the intention was borne out.
  • "Instead,": signals a substitution or reversal, so fails — Duchamp's sculpture confirmed his intention, it did not replace it with something else. ✗
  • "in addition,": signals a new, separate point, so fails — the second sentence is evidence for the first claim, not an independent fact. ✗
  • "Similarly,": signals resemblance between two things, so fails — only one subject (Duchamp's sculpture) is discussed. ✗
  • "Indeed,": signals confirmation or emphasis of what was just claimed, so fits. ✓
This matches the correct answer — D is correct.
